masthead.headline: The week the house started taking notes.
masthead.dek: A short read on what moved, what mattered, and what is worth carrying into next week.

cards.label: In Motion
cards.text: Brand capture, client production, and one internal system finally leaving the whiteboard.

cards.label: Wins
cards.text: A cleaner client review loop and a stronger first pass on the Digest.

cards.label: Milestones
cards.text: Quiet progress across creative, production, partnerships, and ops.

cards.label: Watch
cards.text: A few calendar edges before Thursday reviews. Check the room before you book the room.

feature.title: Audrey is giving the week somewhere to land.
feature.body: The Digest is becoming more than a recap. It is a place for sharp links, small wins, useful context, and the kind of studio knowledge that usually gets buried three threads deep.

images.alt: Halftone blue sky and green hillside
Layered paper and blueprint still life

businessUnits.title: Small notes from the whole house.
businessUnits.lead: The useful stuff is starting to travel.
businessUnits.note: A few things worth knowing from across the studio, pulled out of the feed and put somewhere easier to find.

supportingImage.alt: Layered paper and blueprint editorial still life

notes.text: The Digest is moving from good idea to weekly habit.
Campaign review is tightening around the clearest story, not the longest deck.
The next shoot plan is getting simpler, sharper, and easier to hand off.
Effects language is settling into something people can actually use.
The handoff layer is getting quieter, which is usually how you know it is working.

lessons.title: Leave room for the part people remember.

image.alt: Folded paper, glass of water, pencil, and soft shadow

lessons.body: The fastest version is not always the smallest one. A little structure up front can make the creative part feel less fragile later, and it gives the weird, memorable part a better chance to survive.
lessons.credit: Shared by the house / edited for the issue

sharing.title: Seen in the feed, saved for the house.
sharing.body: A weekly pull from the posts, screenshots, launches, and small public signals worth bringing back into the room.
